pull.body:
Update the Guix time-machine to [576eb72293d0c58bde6fd419916a25382a49159e](https://codeberg.org/guix/guix/commit/576eb72293d0c58bde6fd419916a25382a49159e).

Package updates:
binutils 2.41 -> 2.44
cmake-minimal 3.24.2 -> 3.31.10
diffutils 3.10 -> 3.12
file 5.45 -> 5.46
git-minimal 2.46.0 -> 2.52.0
(base) glibc 2.39 -> 2.41
gzip 1.13 -> 1.14
linux-headers 6.1.119 -> 6.1.164
LLVM/Clang 19.1.4 -> 19.1.7
mingw-w64 12.0.0 -> 13.0.0
nsis 3.10 -> 3.11
python-minimal 3.10 -> 3.11

CMake 4.x becomes available.
Clang/LLVM 20 & 21 become available.

Switch to upstream `osslsigncode` (2.13, `python-lief` (0.17.5) and `python-oscrypto` (1.3.0) packages.

Update glibc to the latest commit on the `2.31` branch.

Could be used for #32764.

Rebased for #34627 and simplified the tar related changes.

> The relevant sub dependency is python-msgspec, which currently has [(supported-systems (list "x86_64-linux" "aarch64-linux"))](https://codeberg.org/guix/guix/src/commit/d15906d2fdc7b8fc5ad40990768dc374cc627b23/gnu/packages/serialization.scm#L767). It's likely this will build fine, and the docs for why the systems are limited, also seem to be outdated.

Eventually got `python-lief` to build with `guix build python-lief --system=riscv64-linux --without-tests=python-pexpect --without-tests=python-pytest-timeout --without-tests=llvm@19 --without-tests=rust@1.93.0 --without-tests=python-trio --without-tests=python-anyio`.
